Auria Update

Wenqing Chen
18.12.2024

Scimap — Spatial count

Spatial analysis based on celltype_2 by using spatial count
celltype_2: 
Tumor_—, Tumor_+--, Tumor_-++, Tumor_--+, Tumor_-+-, Tumor_+++, Tumor_+-+, Tumor_++-
CD4.T.cells, CD8.T.cells, FOXP3.CD4.Tregs, CD68.Macrophages, CD206.Macrophages, Immune, Myofibroblasts, Other

Neighborhoods were computed with r=45px (30microns) and spatial kmeans k=20

‹#›

Key points

After spatial count analysis with 45px radius, it got 11 neighborhoods finally.

NQO1 is basically distributed in tumor cell types, but GCLC+VIM+ has no obvious distribution boundary between tumor and immune cell types.

Based on the spatial count analysis, it’s hard to see whether GCLC+ is highly expressed in CD4 T cell enriched neighborhoods, because GCLC+VIM+ is distributed in various immune and tumor neighborhoods. 

3 cores: The spatial relationships of different cell groups in 'Tumor_GCLC+VIM+_Rich' and 'Tumor_NQO1+_Rich' were demonstrated using spatial distribution and distance measurements. But I don’t find useful information from the distance measurements.

Test the spatial interaction between GCLC+VIM+/NQO1+ tumor cells and other celltypes within a radius of 45px.

NQO1 was especially high in tumor cell-rich neighborhoods

GCLC was especially high in CD4 T cell enriched neighborhood

Different immnue cells showed a higher density in the vicinity of GCLC+VIM+ compared to GCLC-VIM+ or GCLC+VIM-

GCLC+VIM+ cells have a strong association with immune cell populations
